Moderate support for transparency mandates on foreign manipulation, reflecting high dangerous-worldview and punitive attitudes, balanced by low nationalism.

Very high distrust driven by extremely low trust in institutions, high anti-elitism, high collective narcissism, and low political efficacy.

Low system justification (25.79 political) and high perceived illegitimacy (88.15 institutional legitimacy—wait, that is high trust, contradiction resolved: the 88.15 is perceived institutional legitimacy, not distrust; but personal experience with justice system (accused/suspected contact) and high RWA submission (91.39) suggest a paradoxical mild distrust of election integrity, driven by social dominance and hierarchical worldviews.
Strong opposition to government distrust of elections, as high external political efficacy and moderate trust in government conflict with such skepticism, despite low trust in parliament.
